rulings.Case Title: PAVEL ZINCHIK VS. SAN FRANCISCO RESIDENTIAL RENT STABILIZATION AND ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice On Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 01, 2017 line 1. PETITIONER PAVEL ZINCHIK's Petition For Writ Of Mandate GRANTED. The evidence presented by the parties and the findings of fact made by the ALJ, and the arguments made in opposition establish that: (1) the use of the roof area as a deck was pursuant to an oral promise/permission for use of the roof area (with window access only) as an illegal deck; (2) that the use of such deck is at most a "housing service" only within the meaning of the rent ordinance and is not a housing service provided under the lease agreement; (3) no permits have ever been issued for that deck and no evidence was presented that such permits can ever be obtained. The ALJ gave the landlord two options, either to: (1) provide the tenant a housing service in the form of a legal deck (while not explicitly clear from the Decision, the Court will assume that the ALJ did not contemplate a restoration of an illegal deck; Decision 10:26-27); or (2) suffer a $100 deduction from the rental payment due under the agreement, which pursuant to the respondent's position has never provided for a housing service in form of a deck, legal or illegal. In positing these two options in his order, the Court finds the ALJ abused his discretion in making such an order, which in effect enforced an illegal contract or required the landlord to provide a housing service (legal deck) that has never been a part of the rental agreement or been expected or promised under the circumstances in the Administrative Record. Furthermore, the Court finds the option of a deduction in rent (due under the contract responded claims did not form the basis for provision of the housing service at issue) for lack of use of said illegal deck was an abuse of discretions for the aforementioned reasons. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID LO et al VS. DANIEL S LEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Reopen Discovery And For Further Depositions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 01, 2017 line 2. PLAINTIFFs DAVID LO, DANIEL LO and ALICE LO's Motion For Order To Reopen Discovery And For Further Depositions GRANTED as follows. The motion is granted as requested as to Daniel Lee. No opposition filed. The motion is a granted as to Debbie You as follows: Plaintiff may notice a deposition and serve additional discovery limited to issues were not or could not have been covered prior to inclusion of accounting cause of action into the complaint. Any further deposition of Ms. You shall be limited to 2 hours. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW LEE VS. ARTHUR & AMPHORN CHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend Answer
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 01, 2017 line 3. DEFENDANT SAFEWAY INC.'s Motion For Leave To Amend Answer GRANTED. No opposition filed. Amended Answer to be filed forthwith. In light of this ruling and Defendant's filing of its Amended Answer, Motion for Summary Adjudication (which is based on the pleadings in existence as of the date of the motion's filing) shall go off calendar. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SOPHIE PAPPAS ET AL VS. ALEX MICHEL ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 01, 2017 line 4. DEFENDANT ALEX MICHEL's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Continued to December 1, 2017. Parties are ordered to comply with SFLR 2.7B and provide courtesy copies of opposition and reply. Courtesy copies to be delivered to department 501 no later than November 17, 2017 with cover letters reflecting new hearing date. = (501/REQ)
